perturbation.original_text: 1.53 Term. The term shall have the meaning set forth in Section 2.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.53 Term. The term shall have the meaning set forth in Section 15.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Term' is changed to refer to Section 15 instead of Section 2. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 2 defines the commencement and continuation of the agreement, while Section 15 outlines the duration and termination conditions. This makes the 'Term' of the agreement ambiguous: Is it defined by its commencement (Section 2) or its termination (Section 15)?
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 2 TERM 2.01 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue until it is terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 15 of this Agreement ("Term").
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the definition of 'Term' from Section 2 to Section 15. The contradicted text, Section 2, explicitly states that the agreement commences according to its terms and continues until terminated in accordance with Section 15, and then defines 'Term' as that period. By redefining 'Term' as Section 15 only, the modified text contradicts the original meaning and creates ambiguity. Does 'Term' mean the commencement and continuation as described in Section 2, or the termination conditions as described in Section 15? This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the definition of a crucial element of the contract, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: 1.12 Confidential Information. The term shall have the meaning set forth in Section 11.03.1
perturbation.changed_text: 1.12 Confidential Information. The term shall have the meaning set forth in Section 1.01
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Confidential Information' is changed to refer to Section 1.01 (1933 Act definition) instead of Section 11.03.1 (Confidentiality definition). This creates an in-text contradiction because now 'Confidential Information' is being described as The Securities Act of 1933, as amended and this is not how 'Confidential Information' should be defined, thus causing a contradiction and uncerntainty about what the term means.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.02.1 Definition. 11.02.1.1 "Confidential Information" means information obtained from a Party (i) in connection with the development of or performance of any of the Transaction Documents; (ii) concerning customers of the Parties or customers of their Affiliates, including their identities, addresses, and telephone numbers; (iii) as to a Party's or its Affiliate's business methods, operations, or affairs, or the - 20 - processes and systems used in the operation of its or its Affiliate's business; (iv) concerning the Parties and their Affiliates that is identified as confidential by a Party; or (v) required to be treated as confidential under applicable Law. 11.02.1.2 "Confidential Information" does not include (i) information now available in the public domain or that in the future enters the public domain through no fault of the receiving Party; (ii) information disclosed to the receiving Party by a third party without violation by such third party of an independent obligation of confidentiality; (iii) information that is independently developed by or for a Party or its Affiliate in the ordinary course of business outside of any of the Transaction Documents; or (iv) information whose disclosure by the receiving Party is consented to in writing by the disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'Confidential Information' from Section 11.03.1 (Confidentiality definition) to Section 1.01 (1933 Act definition). This directly contradicts Section 11.02.1.1, which provides a detailed definition of 'Confidential Information' related to the transaction documents, customer information, business methods, and legal requirements. The change creates a fundamental incompatibility, as 'Confidential Information' is now defined as something completely different, rendering Section 11.02.1.1 meaningless and unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 1.49 Registration Statement. At any time that this Agreement is in effect, each currently effective registration statement and each currently effective post-effective amendment thereto filed with the SEC under the 1933 Act on Form S-1 or otherwise relating to the GIE including the Prospectus and financial statements included in, and all exhibits to, such registration statement or post- effective amendment prepared by PHL Variable.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.49 Registration Statement. At any time that this Agreement is in effect, each currently effective registration statement and each currently effective post-effective amendment thereto filed with the SEC under the 1934 Act on Form S-1 or otherwise relating to the GIE including the Prospectus and financial statements included in, and all exhibits to, such registration statement or post- effective amendment prepared by PHL Variable.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Registration Statement' is modified to refer to the 1934 Act instead of the 1933 Act. This creates an in-text contradiction in Section 4.09, Section 5.09 and Section 6.09, as those sections refer to the Registration Statement and Prospectus and rely on the assumption that the Registration Statement complies with the 1933 Act, not the 1934 Act. Making Registration statements not following the 1933 Act in the agreement may lead to the statements being seen untruthful.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.09 Registration Statement and Prospectus. The Registration Statement, on the date of which it was declared effective, did not contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact necessary to make the statements therein not misleading in light of the circumstances under which they were made (it being understood that no representation is made with respect to information about ICAS or ICC provided by ICAS or ICC in writing to PHL Variable). All statements in the Registration Statement, on the date of which it was declared - 8 - effective, relating to taxation and tax effects are true in all material respects. The Prospectus or other information contained in the Registration Statement, when disseminated or used after the effective date of the Registration Statement, shall not contain any untrue statement of a material fact or omit to state a material fact necessary to make the statements therein not misleading in light of the circumstances under which they were made (it being understood that no representation is made with respect to information about ICAS or ICC provided by ICAS or ICC in writing to PHL Variable). PHLVIC shall immediately notify ICC in the event PHLVIC believes or has reason to believe that the representations and warranties in this paragraph are no longer true or completely accurate.
perturbation.justification: The modification changes the reference from the 1933 Act to the 1934 Act in the definition of 'Registration Statement'. Section 4.09 relies on the Registration Statement complying with the 1933 Act. By changing the act, the statement in 4.09 that 'The Registration Statement... did not contain any untrue statement of a material fact' becomes potentially untrue, creating a direct contradiction because compliance with the 1933 Act is no longer assured.
